summary: Surah As-Sajda affirms the divine origin of the Qur’an and the creation of the universe, highlighting resurrection and judgment. It contrasts the fate of believers and disbelievers, stressing the reward of Paradise for those who worship, pray, and remain humble, and the punishment for those who deny the truth. The surah recounts past nations’ destruction as a lesson, encourages perseverance and faith, and warns of the consequences of ignoring divine guidance.
